commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a3d9de96924ad716070a8f82221679dd7a7c77b2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `timedOut && fileTarget` warning-suppression branch in `resolveToolErrorWarningPolicy()` plus regression tests for write/edit timeout warning behavior.

PR surface: Source +14, Tests +80. Total +94 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main routes write/edit `lastToolError` values through the mutating-tool warning branch, so a timed-out mutating write with no user-facing failure acknowledgement produces the extra warning payload. I did not run a live channel reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Maintainers should decide or confirm the correct completion signal before repair; automation should not guess between runtime metadata changes, post-write verification, or preserving the warning.

target.title: fix(agents): suppress Write/Edit failed warning on response-timeout false-failure (#55424)
target.body:
## Summary

Users see a visible "⚠️ Write failed" / "⚠️ Edit failed" warning bubble (Feishu, Telegram, and other channels) when the assistant successfully saved a file but the tool ack reply timed out before getting back. Reporter on `v2026.3.13` Windows 11 documented 8 of 8 writes that completed successfully on disk (`Test-Path` + `Get-Item` verified) yet still surfaced the warning to the user, polluting the chat transcript with false-failure noise.

This PR adds a narrow gate in `resolveToolErrorWarningPolicy()` that suppresses the warning **only** for the exact response-timeout false-failure pattern: `lastToolError.timedOut === true` together with a recorded `lastToolError.fileTarget`. Other mutating-tool timeouts, real file failures, and timeouts without a recorded file target keep their existing visible warnings.

## Root Cause

- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.ts:177` — `resolveToolErrorWarningPolicy()` currently treats every mutating-tool `lastToolError` as a candidate warning, with suppression only via the opt-in `messages.suppressToolErrors` config landed earlier this year. The default path therefore emits "Write failed" / "Edit failed" even when the disk mutation is known-completed.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.ts:489` — when `warningPolicy.showWarning` is true, the payload builder appends `⚠️ <Tool summary> failed`, matching the reporter's visible symptom.
- `src/agents/tool-mutation.ts:33` — `FILE_MUTATING_TOOL_NAMES = new Set(["edit", "write"])`. `extractFileTarget()` (line 279) records a `fileTarget` on `lastToolError` **only** for those two tools, making `fileTarget !== undefined` the precise boundary for "this was a file write/edit that completed before the ack timed out."
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts:1043` — tool-error capture writes `timedOut`, `mutatingAction`, `actionFingerprint`, and `fileTarget` onto `lastToolError`; the existing exec-tool detail branch at `payloads.ts:133` already inspects `timedOut === true`, so the codebase has a precedent for using this signal as a routing flag.
- Execution path: file write tool → disk write completes → ack reply timeout → `lastToolError.timedOut=true, fileTarget={path}` recorded → `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` → `resolveToolErrorWarningPolicy` returns `showWarning: true` because the mutating-tool branch fires unconditionally → user sees "⚠️ Write failed".

This follows the conservative direction in clawsweeper's review on #55424:
*"Add a narrow payload warning-policy repair that suppresses response/ack-timeout false warnings only for file `write`/`edit` cases that likely succeeded, while preserving visible warnings for confirmed file failures and non-file mutating failures."*
*"The narrow maintainable fix is to use existing timeout and file-mutation metadata in the payload warning policy, not to add broad mutating-tool suppression, a new config mode, or post-write filesystem verification."*

The closed-unmerged prior attempt #55444 took essentially the same approach via an `isToolResponseTimeoutError()` helper but was lost to stale-label auto-closure on 2026-03-27 before review engagement. This PR uses the `fileTarget` signal that has since landed in `tool-mutation.ts`, which makes the boundary even tighter (file-write tools only, identified by structured metadata rather than an error-string match).

## Changes

- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.ts`: Add an `isFileWriteResponseTimeoutFalseFailure` gate at the top of the mutating-tool decision tree in `resolveToolErrorWarningPolicy()`. The gate fires only when **both** `lastToolError.timedOut === true` AND `lastToolError.fileTarget !== undefined`. The existing `sessions_send` suppression, `messages.suppressToolErrors` opt-in, mutating-tool branch, exec-tool branch, and recoverable-error branch are unchanged. Inline comment cross-references `tool-mutation.ts` `FILE_MUTATING_TOOL_NAMES` and #55444 for reviewer context.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts`: Add 4 regression tests covering:
  1. write + timedOut + fileTarget → no warning (the bug fix)
  2. edit + timedOut + fileTarget → no warning (sibling surface)
  3. write + timedOut, **no** fileTarget → still warns (no over-suppression)
  4. exec + timedOut + mutatingAction → still warns (no exec/cron/message regression)

## Reproduction (before fix)

Pure-JS replication of `resolveToolErrorWarningPolicy()` against current `upstream/main` (HEAD `dcf0941cd6`):

~~~text
FAIL: write+timedOut+fileTarget → expected showWarning=false, got=true
FAIL: edit+timedOut+fileTarget → expected showWarning=false, got=true
PASS: exec+timedOut, no fileTarget → still warns
PASS: write+suppressToolErrors → suppressed (opt-in already works)
~~~

Confirms: the visible failure path (write/edit timeouts with a recorded file target) takes the default-warn mutating-tool branch on current `main`. Only the opt-in `messages.suppressToolErrors` path is currently silent.

## Verification (after fix)

Same replication against the patched logic:

~~~text
PASS: write+timedOut+fileTarget → suppressed
PASS: edit+timedOut+fileTarget → suppressed
PASS: write+timedOut, no fileTarget → still warns
PASS: write, not timed out, fileTarget → still warns
PASS: exec+timedOut, no fileTarget → still warns
PASS: sessions_send → suppressed
PASS: write+suppressToolErrors → suppressed
PASS: write+failureAck → suppressed by ack
PASS: write+fileTarget+real error (not timeout) → warns
RESULTS: 9 pass / 0 fail / 9 total
~~~

The 9 cases cover the bug fix (cases 1-2), all pre-existing branches preserved (cases 3-8), and the boundary case where a real file-write error happens to also have `fileTarget` recorded but `timedOut` is false (case 9 — still warns, because only the timeout combination is treated as a known false-failure).

## Test

- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts` — adds 4 new `it()` cases via the existing `buildPayloads()` test helper. Test pattern matches the surrounding `shows mutating tool errors when assistant output claims success` test at line 454, so no new helper boilerplate.
- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts` — clawsweeper's listed acceptance suite for adjacent payload + tool-error capture behavior. The fix does not touch `tool-mutation.ts` or `pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts` so the `lastToolError.fileTarget` capture side stays unchanged.
- `pnpm tsgo src/agents` — the only new symbol is a local `const` inside `resolveToolErrorWarningPolicy`. No new exports.
- Local vitest could not be executed in this `git worktree`-style linked checkout without a fresh `pnpm install`; CI will run the full agent test suite on this PR.

## Notes

- Scope: single decision-tree gate in `resolveToolErrorWarningPolicy()`. The fix uses structured `fileTarget` metadata that `tool-mutation.ts` already records, not error-string heuristics, so it cannot match unrelated mutating-tool timeouts (exec, message, cron, gateway, sessions, skills, memory). No new config keys, no new types, no API surface change.
- Compatibility: pure addition — only `timedOut + fileTarget` falls into the new no-warning branch. The existing `messages.suppressToolErrors` opt-in path still wins. Real file failures without timeout still warn. Mutating-tool failures with user-facing acknowledgement still get suppressed by the existing ack branch.
- Distinguishing from #55444 (closed unmerged 2026-03-27): #55444 detected the timeout via an `isToolResponseTimeoutError()` error-string matcher (`invoke timed out` / `Response timeout`). This PR uses the structured `lastToolError.fileTarget` field that has since landed in `tool-mutation.ts` (commits `8fb22fdfe24b`), which is a tighter and language-independent boundary. The PR also adds the same-surface regression tests #55444 included.

Closes #55424
